mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.5pp threshold, invalidating the negative earnings revision channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates broad-based economic strength and robust consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day returns in over 60% of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in industrial production reflect supply-side bottlenecks and manufacturing weakness, causing immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during industrial production contractions, or the signal fails to predict direction in > 50% of post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels combined with a stagnant or rising federal funds rate indicate real rate tightening and eroding purchasing power, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation and tight-real-rate signal produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months with positive payroll growth, or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts figure exceeding 1.2 million units signals strong construction demand and wealth effects from rising home values, which stimulates consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of months where housing starts exceed 1.2 million, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates a synchronized supply-demand recovery, reducing recession risk premiums and triggering positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields negative 5-day returns in > 55% of observations during early-cycle phases, or the joint predictive power vanishes in out-of-sample validation.
